definitions: To bind or fasten securely with a rope or string.
To occupy or engage fully, making unavailable for other activities.
To conclude or finish a task or matter.
To obstruct or block something, often traffic or a passage.
examples: She was busy tying up the loose ends of the project.
The meeting is tying up all his morning.
They are tying up the boat to the dock.
The accident is tying up traffic for miles.
